content.problemStatement:
For a given integer array AAA of length NNN, and satisfying 0‚â§Ai<N0 \le A_i < N0‚â§Ai‚Äã<N, and another integer parameter MMM (1‚â§M‚â§N1 \le M \le N1‚â§M‚â§N), define f(A,M)f(A, M)f(A,M) in the following way:


Start with an empty set S={}S = \{\}S={}.
For each MMM length subsequence of AAA, i.e. all sequences of the form [Ai1,Ai2,‚Ä¶,AiM][A_{i_1}, A_{i_2}, \ldots, A_{i_M}][Ai1‚Äã‚Äã,Ai2‚Äã‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AiM‚Äã‚Äã] with 1‚â§i1<i2<‚Ä¶<iM‚â§N1 \le i_1 < i_2 < \ldots < i_M \le N1‚â§i1‚Äã<i2‚Äã<‚Ä¶<iM‚Äã‚â§N, insert the MEX‚ÄÝ^{\dagger}‚ÄÝ of the subsequence into SSS.
f(A,M)f(A, M)f(A,M) is defined as the final size of the set SSS.
Less formally, f(A,M)f(A, M)f(A,M) is the number of possible MEX-values from MMM-length subsequences of AAA.

For example, for A=[0,1,2]A = [0, 1, 2]A=[0,1,2] and M=2M = 2M=2, we will consider the subsequences [0,1],[0,2][0, 1], [0, 2][0,1],[0,2] and [1,2][1, 2][1,2], and the corresponding MEX values 222, 111 and 000. Thus, S={0,1,2}S = \{0, 1, 2\}S={0,1,2} and f(A,M)=3f(A, M) = 3f(A,M)=3.

You are given an integer NNN. For each integer MMM from 111 to NNN:


find the sum of f(A,M)f(A, M)f(A,M) over all **non-decreasing** arrays AAA satisfying the following conditions:

A1‚â§A2‚â§‚Ä¶ANA_1 \le A_2 \le \ldots A_NA1‚Äã‚â§A2‚Äã‚â§‚Ä¶AN‚Äã (the non-decreasing condition)
0‚â§Ai<N0 \le A_i < N0‚â§Ai‚Äã<N


Since the answers can be large, output all of them modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input for each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answers for M=1,2,‚Ä¶,NM = 1, 2, \ldots, NM=1,2,‚Ä¶,N modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
